items.headline: U.S. Stocks Retreat from Record Highs Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Rising Oil Prices
items.summary: U.S. equities edged lower today as markets consolidated after a recent record run, with escalating Middle East tensions and a surge in oil prices dampening investor sentiment. Brent crude climbed 1.6% to $97.51 per barrel, nearing the $100 mark, following fresh developments in the region. Despite the broader market dip, strong corporate earnings and continued optimism around AI and semiconductor stocks provided some underlying support.

items.statContext: Slipped 1% amidst a broader tech slide.
Fell 0.9% as rising oil prices weighed on sentiment.

items.headline: Marvell Technology Rallies on 'Trillion-Dollar Company' Talk
items.summary: Marvell Technology surged after Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang suggested the chipmaker has the potential to grow into a trillion-dollar company.

items.headline: GameStop Jumps on Strong Revenue and Buyback Plan
items.summary: The video-game retailer saw its stock jump after reporting a 14% increase in quarterly revenue and announcing a share buyback program worth up to $2 billion.

items.headline: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Soars on Robust Earnings
items.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise shares surged after the company posted robust earnings, exceeding analysts' expectations, and raised its full-year guidance.

items.headline: Medtronic Climbs on Strong Profit, Dividend Increase
items.summary: Medtronic climbed after reporting a stronger profit for the latest quarter than analysts expected and increasing its dividend payout.

items.headline: Palo Alto Networks Falls Despite Beating Profit Estimates
items.summary: The cybersecurity firm's stock fell despite reporting better-than-expected quarterly profit, as investors may have anticipated even stronger results after its significant year-to-date surge.

items.headline: Alphabet Declines on AI Buildout Funding Plans
items.summary: Shares of Alphabet Inc. fell after the company announced plans to raise $80 billion from stock sales to fund its artificial intelligence buildout.

items.headline: AI Continues to Drive Tech & Industrials, Broader Tech and Cyclicals See Rotation
items.summary: The AI-driven rally remained a significant force, benefiting semiconductor and technology companies tied to data centers and advanced computing. The Information Technology Select Sector SPDR (XLK) gained 1.3%, while the Utilities Select Sector SPDR (XLU) climbed 1.9%. Industrials also emerged as strong performers, riding the 'physical AI supercycle' and ranking as one of the best sectors in the S&P 500 this year, up 11%. However, some segments of the tech, communications, and financial sectors experienced declines. There was a notable rotation out of crowded long-duration technology names into cyclicals, energy, and materials, particularly observed in the Australian market where the tech sector underperformed.

items.headline: U.S. Services Sector Accelerates; Fed Rate Hike Concerns Mount
items.summary: U.S. services sector activity accelerated in May, with the ISM services index rising to 54.5, surpassing market expectations. Despite this, employment contracted for a third consecutive month. Cost pressures remained elevated, driven by tariffs and surging energy costs. Earlier, U.S. job openings in April rose to 7.618 million, reinforcing a hawkish outlook for the Federal Reserve's rate path. The possibility of the Fed raising interest rates this year is growing due to persistent inflation, which was 3.8% in April, and positive jobs data. Federal Reserve governor Michael Barr was scheduled to speak today, with investors also awaiting ADP employment and factory orders data.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports Highlight Corporate Strength
items.summary: Several major companies reported earnings today. Macy's Inc. ($M) announced strong first-quarter 2026 results, exceeding guidance with comparable sales up 3.0%, and subsequently raised its full-year outlook. Medtronic ($MDT) also reported stronger-than-expected profit for the latest quarter and increased its dividend payout. Hewlett Packard Enterprise ($HPE) delivered robust earnings, beating estimates and raising guidance. However, Palo Alto Networks ($PANW) fell despite better-than-expected profit, indicating high investor expectations. Broadcom ($AVGO) had earnings scheduled after the market close.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Drive Oil Surge, Inflationary Pressures
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, including new U.S.-Iran developments, continued to push oil prices higher, with Brent crude nearing $100 per barrel. This ongoing conflict is contributing to higher inflation and increased pressure on the global economy. Concurrently, rising bond yields, with the 10-year U.S. Treasury note climbing to 4.48%, reflect concerns around elevated energy prices and higher borrowing costs. The AI-driven rally, however, remains a strong underlying trend, fueling gains in related technology and industrial sectors.
